metadata.title: Ulysses
metadata.note: Dataset compiled from a public-domain text. Coordinates via OpenStreetMap/Nominatim; routes via OSRM (driving) / BRouter (foot).

properties.name: The Joyce Tower, Sandycove
properties.gloss: Stephen, Mulligan and Haines lodge in the Martello tower; the novel opens on its gun-platform.
properties.quote: Stately, plump Buck Mulligan came from the stairhead, bearing a bowl of lather on which a mirror and a razor lay crossed.

properties.name: The Forty Foot
properties.gloss: The men's bathing place just below the tower, where Mulligan goes down to swim — 'the snotgreen sea, the scrotumtightening sea.'

properties.name: The School, Dalkey
properties.gloss: Mr Deasy's boys' school at Summerfield; Stephen teaches a history class and is paid his wages.

properties.name: Sandymount Strand
properties.gloss: Stephen walks the tideline alone, meditating on perception — the 'ineluctable modality of the visible.'
properties.quote: Ineluctable modality of the visible: at least that if no more, thought through my eyes.

properties.name: No. 7 Eccles Street
properties.gloss: Bloom's morning at home: he fries a kidney and brings Molly her breakfast.
properties.quote: Mr Leopold Bloom ate with relish the inner organs of beasts and fowls.

properties.name: Sir John Rogerson's Quay
properties.gloss: Bloom begins his drifting, lotus-scented morning along the river quays.

properties.name: Westland Row Post Office
properties.gloss: Bloom collects Martha Clifford's letter under the name Henry Flower.

properties.name: Sweny's Pharmacy
properties.gloss: Bloom buys the cake of lemon soap he carries through the day.

properties.name: Paddy Dignam's house
properties.gloss: The funeral procession sets out from the dead man's house in Sandymount.

properties.name: Glasnevin Cemetery
properties.gloss: Dignam's burial; Bloom broods on death and the dead among the graves.

properties.name: Freeman's Journal / Evening Telegraph
properties.gloss: The newspaper offices; the episode of windy rhetoric and headlines.

properties.name: Graham Lemon's sweetshop
properties.gloss: Bloom is handed the 'Elijah is coming' throwaway outside Lemon's, 49 Lower O'Connell Street.

properties.name: Davy Byrne's pub
properties.gloss: Bloom lunches on a gorgonzola sandwich and a glass of burgundy in the 'moral pub.'
properties.quote: He entered Davy Byrne's. Moral pub. He doesn't chat. Stands a drink now and then.

properties.name: National Library of Ireland
properties.gloss: Stephen expounds his theory of Hamlet to the librarians; Bloom passes between the doors.

properties.name: Fr Conmee: Jesuit house
properties.gloss: Father Conmee sets out from the Jesuit house in Gardiner Street on his afternoon walk.

properties.name: Fr Conmee: Marino
properties.gloss: Father Conmee's walk carries him out towards Marino.

properties.name: Blazes Boylan: Thornton's
properties.gloss: Blazes Boylan orders fruit and wine for Molly at Thornton's, 63 Grafton Street.

properties.name: Ned Lambert: St Mary's Abbey
properties.gloss: Ned Lambert shows the historic Chapter House of St Mary's Abbey to a visiting clergyman.

properties.name: Lenehan & M'Coy: Crampton Court
properties.gloss: Lenehan and M'Coy cut through Crampton Court, gossiping about Boylan and Molly.

properties.name: Mr Bloom: Merchant's Arch
properties.gloss: Bloom browses the book-barrows by Merchant's Arch and chooses 'Sweets of Sin' for Molly.

properties.name: Dilly Dedalus: Dillon's auction rooms
properties.gloss: Dilly Dedalus waits outside Dillon's auction rooms, 25 Bachelor's Walk.

properties.name: Mr Kernan: James's Street
properties.gloss: Tom Kernan strolls contentedly down James's Street after a good sale.

properties.name: Stephen Dedalus: Russell's shop
properties.gloss: Stephen lingers at a jeweller's window, Russell's, 57 Fleet Street.

properties.name: Martin Cunningham: Dublin Castle
properties.gloss: Martin Cunningham gathers subscriptions for the Dignam family at the Castle.

properties.name: Buck Mulligan: DBC tearooms
properties.gloss: Mulligan and Haines take tea at the DBC tearooms, 33 Dame Street.

properties.name: Master Dignam: Ruggy O'Donohoe's
properties.gloss: Young Patrick Dignam dawdles past Ruggy O'Donohoe's pub, 23 Wicklow Street.

properties.name: Viceroy's cavalcade: Viceregal Lodge
properties.gloss: The viceregal cavalcade sets out from the Viceregal Lodge in the Phoenix Park.

properties.name: Viceroy's cavalcade: RDS showgrounds
properties.gloss: The cavalcade proceeds through the city to the RDS showgrounds at Ballsbridge.

properties.name: The Ormond Hotel
properties.gloss: The barmaids Miss Douce and Miss Kennedy; music and song fill the 'Sirens' episode.

properties.name: Arbour Hill / Stonybatter
properties.gloss: The nameless narrator crosses Stonybatter on his way to the pub.

properties.name: Barney Kiernan's pub
properties.gloss: Barney Kiernan's, 8–10 Little Britain Street; the Citizen's hostility drives Bloom out.

properties.name: Sandymount Strand
properties.gloss: Gerty MacDowell on the strand at dusk, watched from afar by Bloom.

properties.name: Holles Street Hospital
properties.gloss: Mina Purefoy gives birth; the prose gestates through the history of English style.

properties.name: Mabbot Street (Nighttown)
properties.gloss: Mabbot Street, the entrance to Nighttown (Monto), where the hallucinatory drama begins.

properties.name: Bella Cohen's
properties.gloss: Bella Cohen's brothel, 82 Lower Tyrone Street (now Railway Street).

properties.name: Cabman's shelter, Butt Bridge
properties.gloss: Bloom takes the exhausted Stephen for coffee in the cabman's shelter.

properties.name: Return to Eccles Street
properties.gloss: Bloom and Stephen walk back to Eccles Street; the cosmic catechism of 'Ithaca.'

properties.name: No. 7 Eccles Street
properties.gloss: Molly Bloom's nocturnal monologue in bed — the book's closing 'yes I said yes I will Yes.'
properties.quote: yes I said yes I will Yes.

properties.name: Stephen's morning: to the Forty Foot
properties.gloss: From the Martello tower down to the Forty Foot, where Mulligan swims.

properties.name: Stephen's morning: to the school
properties.gloss: Stephen's way from Sandycove to Mr Deasy's school in Dalkey.

properties.name: Stephen's morning: by rail to Sandymount
properties.gloss: Stephen most likely takes the coastal railway (the Dublin & South Eastern line, today's DART) from Dalkey to Sandymount, then walks onto the strand.

properties.name: Funeral route
properties.gloss: Paddy Dignam's funeral cortège from Sandymount across the city to Glasnevin.

properties.name: Fr Conmee's walk
properties.gloss: Father Conmee walks on foot from the Jesuit house in Gardiner Street out towards Marino.

properties.name: Viceregal cavalcade
properties.gloss: The viceregal cavalcade's progress from the Phoenix Park to the RDS at Ballsbridge.

properties.name: Walk home to Eccles Street
properties.gloss: Bloom and Stephen's late walk from the cabman's shelter at Butt Bridge up Marlborough Street and Temple Street to Eccles Street.
